Traditionally, Diasperus microcladus is used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, cough suppression, and inflammation relief, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Diasperus microcladus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Diasperus microcladus include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory infections and other health issues in traditional medicine.
